key_points: Congruence means exactly the same shape and the same size
Two line segments are congruent if they have the same length
Two angles are congruent if they have the same measure
Correspondence refers to the matching of vertices between two congruent triangles
examples: Congruent line segments: $AB = 5\text{ cm}$ and $CD = 5\text{ cm} \implies AB \cong CD$
Congruent angles: $\angle ABC = 60^{\circ}$ and $\angle PQR = 60^{\circ} \implies \angle ABC \cong \angle PQR$
Congruent squares: Two squares with side length $4\text{ cm}$ each
Superimposition: Placing one biscuit over another from the same pack to see if they match
formulas: Congruence condition $\implies$ Area and Perimeter of both figures are equal
Correspondence notation $\implies$ If $\triangle ABC \cong \triangle PQR$, then $A \leftrightarrow P, B \leftrightarrow Q, C \leftrightarrow R$
exam_tips: Always use the symbol $\cong$ instead of just $=$ for figures
Check both shape and size before declaring congruence
In correspondence, the order of letters is very important
Remember that congruent figures are always similar, but similar figures are not always congruent
